title: ranunculus occidentalis var. alceus
intro: Ranunculus occidentalis var. alceus, also known as Western Buttercup or Alceus Buttercup, is a perennial plant belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as supporting immune function and wound healing. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus occidentalis var. alceus can be used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to note that proper identification and dosing are crucial due to the plant's potential toxicity.
